description: The Luddite movement in England between 1811 and 1816 involved textile workers destroying wide-frame machinery they believed threatened their livelihoods. The British government eventually sided with industrial owners, deploying 12,000 troops to suppress the rebellion.

key_events.description: First systematic frame breaking at Arnold, Nottinghamshire.
Government introduces the Frame Breaking Act (1812), making it a capital offense.
Mass trial and execution of Luddites at York Castle.
The movement effectively collapses under military pressure.

calibration_notes: Measures extreme radicalization and the impact of state intervention on worker resistance to automation.
